name: 03 - Realistic Hard Suite
description: Real-data business questions at the hard level — multi-table joins, anti-joins, HAVING, computed metrics. Every scenario has ExpectedSql.

Scenarios.Question: Compare ticket counts between this month and last month.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT 'ThisMonth' AS Period, COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE YEAR(CreatedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE()) AND MONTH(CreatedAt) = MONTH(GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 UNION ALL SELECT 'LastMonth', COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE YEAR(CreatedAt) = YEAR(DATEADD(month, -1, GETDATE())) AND MONTH(CreatedAt) = MONTH(DATEADD(month, -1, GETDATE())) AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Compare ticket volume this year versus last year.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT 'CurrentYear' AS Period, COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets WHERE YEAR(CreatedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE()) AND IsDeleted = 0 UNION ALL SELECT 'LastYear', COUNT(*) FROM Tickets WHERE YEAR(CreatedAt) = YEAR(GETDATE()) - 1 AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Show me tickets per user — full name, their entity, and the number of tickets they created.
Scenarios.Category: Hard / Tickets per user with entity
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS UserName, e.Name AS Entity, COUNT(*) AS Tickets FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId LEFT JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = u.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName, e.Name ORDER BY Tickets DESC

Scenarios.Question: For each agent, show the number of tickets assigned and average resolution time in hours.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS Agent, COUNT(*) AS Assigned, AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionHours FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.AssignedToUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY Assigned DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which tickets have no comments yet?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN TicketComments c ON c.TicketId = t.Id WHERE c.Id IS NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Which users haven't created any tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.Id, u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS UserName, u.Email FROM AspNetUsers u LEFT JOIN Tickets t ON t.CreatedByUserId = u.Id WHERE t.Id IS NULL

Scenarios.Question: What is the average resolution time in hours for resolved tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT AVG(CAST(DATEDIFF(hour, CreatedAt, ResolvedAt) AS FLOAT)) AS AvgResolutionHours FROM Tickets WHERE ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Who are the top 5 users by tickets created?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 5 u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS UserName, COUNT(*) AS Tickets FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY Tickets DESC

Scenarios.Question: Who are the top 5 agents by tickets resolved?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 5 u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS Agent, COUNT(*) AS Resolved FROM Tickets t JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.ResolvedByUserId WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY u.FirstName, u.LastName ORDER BY Resolved DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show me tickets that breached their SLA along with their priority and who created them.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, p.Name AS Priority, u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS CreatedBy FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketPriorities p ON p.Id = t.PriorityId JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.CreatedByUserId WHERE t.IsSlaBreached = 1 AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: Which categories have more than 15 tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.Name AS Category, COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY c.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 15 ORDER BY Total DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which entities have more than 10 tickets?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT e.Name AS Entity, COUNT(*) AS Total FROM Tickets t JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = t.EntityId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY e.Name HAVING COUNT(*) > 10 ORDER BY Total DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show me tickets that took more than 24 hours to resolve.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS Hours FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) > 24 AND t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY Hours DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which 10 tickets have the most comments?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, COUNT(c.Id) AS Comments FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketComments c ON c.TicketId = t.Id WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title ORDER BY Comments DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show pending tickets along with their pending reason.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.PendingReason FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Pending' AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: List all end-users with their entity name.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS UserName, e.Name AS Entity FROM AspNetUsers u JOIN Entitys e ON e.Id = u.EntityId JOIN AspNetUserRoles ur ON ur.UserId = u.Id JOIN AspNetRoles r ON r.Id = ur.RoleId WHERE r.Name = 'EndUser' AND u.IsActive = 1 ORDER BY e.Name, u.FirstName

Scenarios.Question: Show escalated tickets with the escalation level and who they were escalated to.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, t.EscalationLevel, u.FirstName + ' ' + u.LastName AS EscalatedTo FROM Tickets t LEFT JOIN AspNetUsers u ON u.Id = t.EscalatedToUserId WHERE t.EscalatedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0

Scenarios.Question: How many closed tickets does each category have?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT c.Name AS Category, COUNT(*) AS ClosedTotal FROM Tickets t JOIN TicketCategories c ON c.Id = t.CategoryId JOIN TicketStatuses s ON s.Id = t.StatusId WHERE s.Name = 'Closed' AND t.IsDeleted = 0 GROUP BY c.Name ORDER BY ClosedTotal DESC

Scenarios.Question: Which 10 tickets took the longest to resolve?
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT TOP 10 t.Id, t.TicketNumber, t.Title, DATEDIFF(hour, t.CreatedAt, t.ResolvedAt) AS Hours FROM Tickets t WHERE t.ResolvedAt IS NOT NULL AND t.IsDeleted = 0 ORDER BY Hours DESC

Scenarios.Question: Show child tickets with their parent ticket number and title.
Scenarios.ExpectedSql: SELECT t.Id AS ChildId, t.TicketNumber AS ChildNumber, t.Title AS ChildTitle, p.TicketNumber AS ParentNumber, p.Title AS ParentTitle FROM Tickets t JOIN Tickets p ON p.Id = t.ParentTicketId WHERE t.IsDeleted = 0
